metadata.title: User Management Feature: Multi-Tenant Integration Summary
metadata.author: Claude Code

summary.purpose: Document integration between new User Management feature and recently completed Multi-Tenant Foundation (MT-M1 through MT-M5)
summary.context: User requested 'integrate with the recent work on the database structure for multi tenanncy' and create source doc for strategic planning
summary.outcome: Complete alignment verified, FEATURE.json created following roadmap schema patterns, ready for strategic execution

integration: Extends existing Tenant model with trial tracking fields (trial_started_at, trial_ends_at, converted_at)
Extends existing User model with registration fields (email_verified, email_verified_at, last_login_at)
Leverages existing tenant_id foreign keys and indexes
Uses existing TenantStatus and SubscriptionTier enums
Builds on existing StakeholderRole enum for role assignments
UserAuthorizationService uses TenantContextService.getCurrentTenantId() for request-scoped isolation
TenantProvisioningService uses runWithTenant() for atomic tenant creation workflow
All services leverage queryWithTenantContext() for RLS session variable injection
Registration flow uses transactionWithTenantContext() for multi-query isolation
Authorization relies on RLS policies with app.current_tenant_id session variable
User table RLS policy allows platform admins cross-tenant access (is_platform_admin=true)
New UserInvitation/UserRegistration tables will have RLS policies added in M1
Audit logs automatically include tenant_id for compliance queries
Admin UI extends existing 16 tenant management endpoints
Adds user management endpoints alongside tenant CRUD
Leverages existing admin authentication middleware
Reuses tenant validation and status control patterns
Follows same migration runbook pattern for AUTHORIZED_USERS → database migration
Uses atomic transaction approach from tenant migration scripts
Applies zero-downtime deployment strategy (blue-green)
Leverages existing seed script patterns (database/seed-multi-tenant-demo.sql)

multiTenantFoundation.alignmentVerification: 100% - All MT-M1 through MT-M5 deliverables are utilized by User Management feature

alignment.implementation: User Management M2: TenantProvisioningService executes exact workflow
workflow: 1. Create tenant record in tenants table
2. Generate tenant-specific schema: CREATE SCHEMA tenant_{slug}
3. Run schema migrations: Prisma migrate deploy

alignment.implementation: User Management M2: UserAuthorizationService sets session variables

variables.app.current_tenant_id: Set from authenticated user's tenant_id during request
variables.app.is_platform_admin: Set from user.is_platform_admin flag for cross-tenant access
variables.app.current_user_id: Set from JWT claims for user-level RLS policies

capabilities.dataResidency: Tenant provisioning respects region (Canadian pilot in tor1)
capabilities.breachNotification: Audit trail enables 72-hour breach notification requirement

alignment.implementation: User Management M1 adds user-focused seed scripts

identified.impact: Blocks M3 (email verification) and M5 (automated emails)
identified.recommendation: Provision SendGrid or AWS SES before M3 start (target: Feb 10)

identified.gap: Domain verification for email (DKIM/SPF/DMARC) not configured
identified.impact: Verification emails may be marked as spam
identified.recommendation: Configure DNS records during email service setup

oauth.replacement: User Management M6: UserAuthorizationService with Redis cache
oauth.migrationStrategy: Blue-green deployment with overlap period to ensure zero authentication failures
oauth.riskMitigation: Comprehensive E2E testing, dry-run migration, rollback plan documented

waitlist.currentFlow: Waitlist table insert → n8n forward → Twenty CRM lead creation
waitlist.enhancement: User Management M5: Bidirectional sync with lifecycle stages
waitlist.newStages: waitlist → email_verified → tenant_created → trial → paid_customer → churned
waitlist.backwardCompatible: Existing waitlist entries automatically upgraded to new lifecycle tracking

admin.currentCapabilities: Demo scenarios, system metrics, audit log viewing
admin.additions: User Management M4: User CRUD, Tenant CRUD, Role assignment, Invitation management, User impersonation

analytics.funnelMetrics: Waitlist → Trial conversion, Trial → Paid conversion

schemaEvolution.foundation: Multi-Tenant Foundation provides base schema (Tenant, User, RLS policies)
schemaEvolution.extension: User Management adds 4 new models without changing foundation
schemaEvolution.approach: Additive schema changes only - no breaking changes to existing models
schemaEvolution.migrationStrategy: Standard Prisma migrate workflow, tested in staging before production

serviceLayers.existing: TenantContextService (from MT-M2) provides request-scoped tenant isolation
serviceLayers.new: 5 new services (Registration, Invitation, Provisioning, Authorization, CRM) all use TenantContextService
serviceLayers.integration: All new services are tenant-aware by default, leveraging AsyncLocalStorage pattern
serviceLayers.transactionSafety: All provisioning operations use Prisma $transaction() with automatic rollback

securityModel.authentication: No changes - continues using Google OAuth via Passport.js
securityModel.authorization: Upgrade from env var to database-driven with RLS enforcement
securityModel.sessionManagement: Redis-backed sessions with automatic regeneration (existing pattern)
securityModel.auditTrail: All user/tenant operations logged in existing audit_logs table with tenant_id

apiDesign.pattern: RESTful endpoints following existing route structure
apiDesign.validation: Zod schemas for request/response (consistent with existing endpoints)
apiDesign.errorHandling: Custom error types extending existing ApiError base class

alignment.enabledBy: User Management M1-M3 (basic registration by Feb 15)
alignment.impact: Pilot customer can self-service register, no manual SSH provisioning required

alignment.enabledBy: User Management M1-M6 complete + revenue traction
alignment.impact: Demonstrates automated customer acquisition, reduces investor risk

alignment.enabledBy: User Management + CRM integration + subscription management
alignment.impact: Automated waitlist → trial → paid conversion, scalable growth model

revenueGeneration.current: $0 MRR (no paying customers, manual onboarding blocks growth)
revenueGeneration.enabler: User Management removes manual provisioning bottleneck, enables self-service signup
revenueGeneration.scalingPath: Automated onboarding enables unlimited customer growth without operational scaling

operationalImpact.before: 2 hours/week platform admin time on manual user provisioning, SSH access required
operationalImpact.after: 0 hours/week - fully automated self-service registration and tenant provisioning
operationalImpact.scalability: Manual pattern limited to ~5 new customers/month, automated enables unlimited growth

infrastructureReadiness.redis: Sufficient capacity - authorization cache <10MB for 1000 users
infrastructureReadiness.apiGateway: Sufficient capacity - current load <10% of capacity
infrastructureReadiness.networking: Sufficient - current firewall rules allow GitHub Actions + production droplets

testingStrategy.unit: 25+ unit tests per service class (M2 deliverable)
testingStrategy.integration: 11+ integration tests for API endpoints (M3 deliverable)
testingStrategy.e2e: 15+ Playwright tests for admin workflows (M4 deliverable)
testingStrategy.load: k6 scenarios for registration flow (optional, post-M6)

critical.risk: March 2026 pilot customer delayed due to incomplete user management
critical.mitigation: M1-M3 target Feb 15 (2 weeks buffer before March 1 pilot), basic registration sufficient for pilot
critical.contingency: Manual provisioning available as fallback for single pilot customer

critical.mitigation: Blue-green deployment, comprehensive E2E testing, dry-run migration, rollback plan
critical.contingency: Instant rollback to env var pattern, <5 min downtime

medium.risk: Email deliverability issues block registration flow
medium.mitigation: Use reputable email service (SendGrid/AWS SES), proper DKIM/SPF/DMARC setup, test deliverability pre-launch
medium.contingency: Manual email verification fallback, alternative email service provider

medium.risk: CRM integration failures cause registration delays
medium.mitigation: CRM sync is async/non-blocking, retry logic with exponential backoff, fallback to manual sync
medium.contingency: Registration succeeds even if CRM sync fails, manual cleanup possible

low.risk: Redis cache failures degrade authorization performance
low.mitigation: Fallback to database query if Redis unavailable, monitoring for cache hit rate
low.impact: Performance degradation (100ms → 200ms p95 latency) but no authentication failures

technical.target: <5 minutes p95 (signup → email verify → profile complete → tenant created)

business.measurement: Stripe subscription data + CRM opportunity tracking

operational.metric: Platform admin time on user provisioning

immediate.task: Review FEATURE.json with stakeholders for approval
immediate.outcome: Approval to proceed with M1 implementation

immediate.task: Provision email service (SendGrid or AWS SES)

shortTerm.deliverable: 4 new models, migration tested on staging

planningDocuments.purpose: Primary strategic planning document following roadmap schema
planningDocuments.content: 6 milestones, deliverables, success criteria, business value, dependencies

planningDocuments.purpose: Integration analysis with multi-tenant foundation and database strategy
planningDocuments.content: Alignment verification, integration points, risk analysis, success metrics

planningDocuments.purpose: Detailed technical architecture and implementation specifications
planningDocuments.content: Service designs, API endpoints, database models, security considerations

implementationDocuments.purpose: Step-by-step migration from env var to database authorization
OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for user management endpoints
User guide for platform admins managing users/tenants

conclusion: User Management feature is fully aligned with recently completed Multi-Tenant Foundation (MT-M1 through MT-M5) and Database Strategy. All integration points verified, dependencies satisfied (except email service provisioning), and implementation roadmap defined with 6 milestones targeting March 1, 2026 completion. Feature is CRITICAL priority for enabling March 2026 pilot customer onboarding and Sept 2026 revenue generation milestone ($5K-$10K MRR). Ready for stakeholder approval and M1 implementation kickoff.
